---
title: Plotting
---

`q.plot` renders interactive Plotly figures for return streams — quick column
charts, equity curves, drawdowns, calendar heatmaps, full quantstats-style
tearsheets, and Monte Carlo diagnostics — and for trade logs in qrt's
canonical trades format (entry/exit markers on price, excursion scatters,
per-trade return distributions), with sensible defaults for quant research.
Return-stream *statistics* (performance, alpha/beta, rolling diagnostics,
`q.stats.montecarlo`, ...) live in `q.stats`; see
[Return Statistics](../stats/index.qmd). For feature engineering and
price-chart examples, see [Feature Engineering](../feature/index.qmd).

Pages:

- [Return charts](returns.ipynb) — column charts, equity curves, drawdowns,
  full performance reports, and monthly heatmaps.
- [Tearsheet report](report.ipynb) — full quantstats-style strategy tearsheet
  (`q.plot.report`) plus its individual panels (metrics table, cumulative
  returns, EOY returns, rolling diagnostics, worst drawdowns, ...).
- [Simulation tests](simulation.ipynb) — Monte Carlo fan charts and outcome
  distributions, variance testing, and noise testing.
- [Trade logs](trades.ipynb) — trade markers on price, MAE/MFE excursions,
  per-trade return distributions, and the trades → tearsheet bridge.
- [Saving figures](saving.qmd) — exporting any figure to HTML/PNG.
